def test_knapsack_exact_capacity_fill():
    # Test case where optimal solution exactly fills capacity
    # capacity=3, items=[(3, 10), (1, 5)]
    # Optimal: take item with weight=3, value=10 (exactly fills capacity)
    # Buggy code: weight < j means when weight == j (3 == 3), it won't consider taking the item
    # because j starts from 1 to capacity, and when j=3, weight=3, condition 3 < 3 is False
    capacity = 3
    items = [(3, 10), (1, 5)]
    expected = 10  # Take the first item exactly filling the knapsack
    
    from source import knapsack
    assert knapsack(capacity, items) == expected, f"Expected {expected}, got {knapsack(capacity, items)}"
